records.ety: [F. cajoler, orig., to chatter like a bird in a cage, to sing; hence, to amuse with idle talk, to flatter, from the source of OF. goale, jaiole, F. gele, dim. of cage a cage. See Cage, Jail.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Cajoled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Cajoling.]

defs: To deceive with flattery or fair words; to wheedle.
quotes: I am not about to cajole or flatter you into a reception of my views.
